quick_skim.title: QSVideo: Query-Conditioned Semantic Temporal Retrieval for Video Understanding
quick_skim.abstract: The performance of vision-language models (VLMs) in video understanding declines with increasing video duration, as video moments unrelated to the query confuse their language components. Multimodal retrieval has emerged as a critical component of video understanding, addressing this challenge by localizing key visual evidence. However, existing multimodal retrieval methods suffer from biased relevance estimation, limited diversity, and temporal collapse. In this paper, we propose QSVideo, a unified framework that systematically addresses relevance, diversity, and temporal modeling in video retrieval. We first introduce a query-conditioned semantic ranker, QSRanker, which reformulates arbitrary questions into retrieval-friendly queries and estimates structured relevance along object, action, and location dimensions. Building upon this, we design QSRetrieval to jointly optimize relevance and diversity for more informative frame selection. Moreover, we propose temporal alignment strategies tailored for both long and streaming videos to improve evidence recall. Extensive experiments on long and streaming video benchmarks demonstrate that QSVideo greatly enhances video VLM performance under strict frame limit constraints. The code is available at https://github.com/human-analysis/QSVideo.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用语言进行查询条件视频检索
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用语言进行查询条件视频检索
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用语言进行查询条件视频检索
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: QSVideo proposes QSRanker to reformulate questions into retrieval-friendly queries for video understanding, addressing biased relevance and temporal collapse.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对视频理解中长视频语言成分混淆的问题，QSVideo提出了一种统一的视频检索框架，通过查询条件语义排序器QSRanker将任意问题重写为检索友好查询，系统解决相关性偏差、多样性不足和时间塌缩问题。该方法提升了视频语言模型对长视频的理解能力。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对视频理解中长视频语言成分混淆的问题，QSVideo提出了一种统一的视频检索框架，通过查询条件语义排序器QSRanker将任意问题重写为检索友好查询，系统解决相关性偏差、多样性不足和时间塌缩问题。该方法提升了视频语言模型对长视频的理解能力。

quick_skim.title: Repurposing CLIP to Localize at Pixel Level
quick_skim.abstract: Large-scale Vision-Language Models like CLIP have demonstrated impressive open-set localization capabilities at the image level. However, adapting this capability to pixel-level dense prediction poses challenges due to global feature biases. In this paper, we introduce CLIPix, a simple yet effective framework that repurposes CLIP to perform pixel-level localization. By tracing back CLIP's classification process, CLIPix identifies object-specific attentive regions and repurposes them as pixel-level localization cues. To address noise introduced by global biases, we propose a Noise-Resistant Correction strategy, refining these cues for more precise segmentation. Additionally, we introduce a Localization Embedding strategy to integrate both localization and enriched detail information, enabling accurate, high-resolution segmentation. Our approach preserves CLIP's generalization strength and unlocks its potential for segmenting arbitrary objects. Extensive experiments on the PASCAL and COCO datasets demonstrate that CLIPix achieves state-of-the-art performance, underscoring its effectiveness.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: CLIPix repurposes CLIP for pixel-level localization via tracing classification process and noise-resistant correction, enabling open-vocabulary dense prediction that supports vision-language tracking tasks.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出CLIPix框架，通过回溯CLIP分类过程提取对象注意力区域，并采用抗噪声校正策略实现像素级定位。该框架无需额外训练即可将CLIP的图像级定位能力拓展至像素级，为视觉语言跟踪中的开放词汇目标定位提供了高效工具，显著降低了密集预测的标注依赖。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文提出CLIPix框架，通过回溯CLIP分类过程提取对象注意力区域，并采用抗噪声校正策略实现像素级定位。该框架无需额外训练即可将CLIP的图像级定位能力拓展至像素级，为视觉语言跟踪中的开放词汇目标定位提供了高效工具，显著降低了密集预测的标注依赖。

quick_skim.title: Segmentation before Answering: Pixel Grounding for MLLM Visual Reasoning
quick_skim.abstract: Recent advancements in Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) have evolved from static perception to interleaved visual-language reasoning, often referred to as ``thinking with images''. A basic operation in this reasoning process is to zoom in on regions of interest (often represented with bounding boxes) to acquire finer visual details. In this paper, we propose \textbf{Seg}mentation before \textbf{Answer}ing (SegAnswer), which shifts the unit of zoom-in from the popular bounding box to pixel-level segmentation mask. By employing fine-grained masks to isolate the target area from cluttered environments, segmented visual input yields a more precise region of interest, effectively filtering out redundant background and interfering objects. Furthermore, the discrete patches of segmented visual input align more seamlessly with how MLLMs structure visual tokens via positional embeddings. In experiments, we evaluate SegAnswer across diverse benchmarks, including high-resolution perception, general perception, and hallucination. It achieves consistent improvements and also exhibits considerable performance on segmentation tasks, validating its capability for reliable pixel grounding.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 像素级定位用于视觉推理
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 像素级定位用于视觉推理
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 像素级定位用于视觉推理
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: SegAnswer uses pixel-level segmentation masks instead of bounding boxes to isolate target regions, improving visual reasoning in MLLMs, which can be transferred to vision-language tracking for precise localization.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出SegAnswer方法，将多模态大模型中的区域聚焦从边界框升级为像素级分割掩码，通过细粒度分割过滤背景干扰，提升视觉推理的精确性。该方法可直接应用于视觉语言跟踪中的目标定位，为语言引导的跟踪提供更精准的候选区域，是视觉语言跟踪领域的一项关键使能技术。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文提出SegAnswer方法，将多模态大模型中的区域聚焦从边界框升级为像素级分割掩码，通过细粒度分割过滤背景干扰，提升视觉推理的精确性。该方法可直接应用于视觉语言跟踪中的目标定位，为语言引导的跟踪提供更精准的候选区域，是视觉语言跟踪领域的一项关键使能技术。

quick_skim.title: VocaDet: Sample-Driven Open-Vocabulary Object Detection and Segmentation via Visual Tokenization and Vector Database Retrieval
quick_skim.abstract: Open-vocabulary object detection and segmentation aim to recognize arbitrary objects beyond predefined categories. Although recent vision-language and reference-based approaches have significantly advanced this field, they often rely on text prompts, limited visual examples, or expensive feature matching procedures, making them difficult to scale to large and continuously expanding object repositories. In this work, we propose VocaDet, a sample-driven open-vocabulary object detection and segmentation framework that learns object concepts directly from user-provided positive and negative sample collections without model retraining. The key idea is to transform continuous visual representations into discrete visual vocabularies and perform efficient retrieval-based recognition through a scalable vector database. Specifically, we employ DINOv3 as the visual feature extractor and apply agglomerative clustering with adaptive clustering sensitivity to generate multi-granularity visual tokens. These visual tokens, together with position-debiased representations and spatial topology information, are stored as expandable object memories in a vector database. During inference, query images are converted into visual tokens and efficiently matched against the stored object memories for object localization and segmentation. Furthermore, a background filtering mechanism is introduced to remove frequently occurring background patterns and reduce redundant retrieval operations in practical fixed-camera scenarios. Experiments on the UA-DETRAC dataset demonstrate that VocaDet achieves effective open-vocabulary detection performance without conventional detector training, while supporting continuously expandable recognition capability as additional positive and negative samples are accumulated.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: open-vocabulary object detection and segmentation using visual tokenization
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用视觉标记化的开放词汇目标检测与分割
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用视觉标记化的开放词汇目标检测与分割
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用视觉标记化的开放词汇目标检测与分割
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: VocaDet learns object concepts from user-provided samples without retraining via visual tokenization and vector database retrieval for open-vocabulary detection and segmentation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对开放词汇目标检测与分割依赖文本提示或昂贵特征匹配的问题，VocaDet提出样本驱动的框架，通过视觉标记化和向量数据库检索直接从正负样本集合学习概念，无需模型重训。实验表明其可扩展到大型物体库，检测质量优异。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对开放词汇目标检测与分割依赖文本提示或昂贵特征匹配的问题，VocaDet提出样本驱动的框架，通过视觉标记化和向量数据库检索直接从正负样本集合学习概念，无需模型重训。实验表明其可扩展到大型物体库，检测质量优异。
